title: PCIe (PCI Express)
description: The high-speed serial bus that connects NICs, GPUs, and storage devices to the CPU and RAM.
fullExplanation: PCIe (PCI Express) is the standard interface connecting expansion cards (NICs, GPUs, NVMe SSDs) to the CPU and memory. PCIe uses serial lanes — each lane provides ~1 GB/s bidirectional bandwidth. NICs use x1, x4, or x8 slots. A 100GbE NIC requires ~12.5 GB/s — needs PCIe 3.0 x8 or PCIe 4.0 x4. PCIe also provides the DMA path — the NIC uses PCIe to read/write system RAM directly.
keyFacts: PCIe is full-duplex — bandwidth is per direction
10GbE NIC: needs PCIe 2.0 x4 (2 GB/s) — 10Gbps = 1.25 GB/s
100GbE NIC: needs PCIe 3.0 x8 (7.88 GB/s) — 100Gbps = 12.5 GB/s
SR-IOV: NIC presents multiple virtual PCIe functions — VMs get direct hardware access
IOMMU: maps NIC's DMA addresses — prevents NIC from accessing unauthorized memory
PCIe hot-plug: add/remove cards without rebooting (server feature)
NVMe SSDs also use PCIe — same bus as NICs, competing for bandwidth
